content: Mongoose pre/post hooks: pre-hooks run in registration order, each awaited before the next. If a pre-hook throws, subsequent hooks and the operation are skipped. Post-hooks run after the operation completes with access to the result. insertMany does not trigger per-document middleware by default. The feathersjs/hooks library implements the canonical onion-layer pattern: each hook is async(ctx, next) where code before await next() is pre-phase and code after is post-phase; context carries method name, args, result, error. Error short-circuit: setting ctx.result before next() skips the original function; throwing aborts the pipeline.
